text.title: Go HTTP Routing
text.summary: Gin and Echo router setup, middleware composition, request binding with validation tags.

rule.statement: Use Gin with small, single-purpose middlewares. Never put business logic in middleware.
rule.rationale: Small middlewares compose cleanly and are easier for agents to understand and extend.

rule.statement: Use ShouldBindJSON for Gin (returns error) instead of BindJSON (aborts on error). Use binding struct tags for validation.
rule.rationale: ShouldBind lets handlers return custom error responses; BindJSON writes a 400 and aborts, bypassing the ErrorHandler middleware.

rule.statement: Use a single ErrorHandler middleware that maps AppError to HTTP status. Handlers call c.Error(err) and return — never call c.JSON directly for errors.
rule.rationale: Centralizing error mapping ensures consistent error response shape and avoids duplicating HTTP status logic in every handler.
